purpose: Source registry for concentric-eyewall and eyewall-replacement observations.

latency.preliminary: around one week after storm dissipation
latency.final: several months after the hemisphere season

tc_primed.scientific_semantics: Intercalibrated passive-microwave brightness temperature and precipitation structure. Final files use final best-track centers; preliminary files use operational best-track centers.
known_limits: The product supplies brightness temperatures rather than an ERC label or a surface-wind secondary maximum.
Coverage fraction is calculated from the S1 geolocation swath within 750 km and does not guarantee inner-core coverage in every channel.
The dataset deliberately omits best-track RMW because that field lacks historical post-season quality control.

nasa_pps_l1c_nrt.latency: near real time; most recent two weeks retained in the NRT endpoint
nasa_pps_l1c_nrt.scientific_semantics: Raw multisensor Level-1C brightness temperatures for operational storm-centered extraction.

archer_mperc.latency: operational updates following adequate microwave overpasses
archer_mperc.scientific_semantics: ARCHER evaluates 85-92 GHz circular convective structure; M-PERC diagnoses ERC-onset probability from ring-score evolution and intensity history.
known_limits: Public storm folders expose probabilities and diagnostic images; a stable public numeric ring-score vector endpoint was not found in this audit.
Microwave overpasses are irregular and a full 24-hour predictor history is required by M-PERC.

cyclobs_sar.format: CSV-like API metadata and NetCDF-4 wind products
cyclobs_sar.latency: data driven; target delivery within one hour of source-SAR availability
cyclobs_sar.scientific_semantics: Instantaneous ocean 10 m wind field on a 3 km effective-resolution product; cyclone-polar files use 1 km radial and 1 degree azimuthal sampling.
known_limits: SAR acquisitions are sparse and mission-planning dependent.
Rain, retrieval physics, swath geometry, center quality, and azimuthal coverage remain part of the observation error model.

hursat_archer_eye.scientific_semantics: Infrared-derived eye geometry for supplementary pretraining and climatology.
